title: Mixture of In-Context Experts Enhance LLMs' Long Context Awareness

method.name: Mixture of In-Context Experts (MoICE)
method.description: A novel plug-in for LLMs that integrates a router within each attention head
key_features: Freezes LLM parameters and exclusively updates routers for only a few steps
Router selects several of the most suitable RoPE angles for each token
Selected RoPE angles collectively contribute to computing attention scores
advantages: Better performance compared to prior methods

problem_addressed.description: LLMs' contextual awareness is not as powerful as widely believed, constraining their application in tasks demanding extensive contextual awareness
specific_issues: Lost-in-middle phenomenon: LLMs exhibit weaker awareness of information in the middle of long context
RoPE (Rotary Positional Embedding) has mathematical properties that impede attention to specific positions in long context

prior_methods.name: Attention Buckets (AB)
prior_methods.description: Enhances context awareness by executing N inference instances with distinct RoPE angles and aggregating outputs

prior_methods.name: Ms-PoE
prior_methods.description: Enhances utility of position-aware heads by re-scaling positional embedding indices
drawbacks: Needs additional forward pass for RoPE angle assignment
Limited awareness of certain contextual positions
Static assignment of RoPE angle for each attention head throughout generation

results.comparison: MoICE demonstrates superior memory efficiency and performance compared to AB and Ms-PoE
results.visualization: Figure 1 illustrates the approaches, showing MoICE's advantage over AB and Ms-PoE
